content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 19 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 109r–110v (LC PDF pp. 216–217) | **Source Images:** DEU_019_page_001.jpg, DEU_019_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. 155 (pa_0155)

---

**1** When the LORD your God cuts off the nations whose land the LORD your God is giving you, and you dispossess them and settle in their cities and in their houses, **2** you shall set apart three cities for yourself in the midst of your land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess. **3** You shall prepare the road for yourself and divide into three parts the territory of your land that the LORD your God allots to you, so that every manslayer may flee there.

**4** Now this is the case of the manslayer who shall flee there and live: whoever strikes his neighbor without knowledge, and he did not hate him from yesterday or the day before— **5** as when a man goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood, and his hand swings the axe to fell the tree, and the iron head slips from the wood and strikes his neighbor so that he dies—he shall flee to one of these cities and live, **6** lest the avenger of blood pursue the manslayer while his heart is hot and overtake him, because the way is long, and strike him fatally—though he was not deserving of death, since he did not hate him from yesterday or the day before. **7** Therefore I command you: You shall set apart three cities for yourself.

**8** And if the LORD your God enlarges your territory, as he swore to your fathers, and gives you all the land that he promised to give to your fathers— **9** provided that you keep all this commandment that I am commanding you today, to love the LORD your God and to walk in his ways always—then you shall add three more cities for yourself in addition to these three, **10** so that innocent blood may not be shed in the midst of your land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, and so that bloodguilt not be upon you.

**11** But if a man hates his neighbor and lies in wait for him and rises against him and strikes him fatally so that he dies, and he flees to one of these cities, **12** then the elders of his city shall send and take him from there and hand him over to the avenger of blood, so that he may die. **13** Your eye shall not pity him; you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, so that it may go well with you.

**14** You shall not move your neighbor's boundary marker, which the former generations have set, in the inheritance that you will inherit in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.

**15** A single witness shall not rise against a man for any iniquity or for any sin, in connection with any sin that he commits; on the evidence of two witnesses or on the evidence of three witnesses shall a matter be confirmed. **16** If a malicious witness rises against a man to accuse him of wrongdoing, **17** then both men who have the dispute shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who are in office in those days. **18** The judges shall inquire diligently, and if the witness is a false witness and has brought a false charge against his brother, **19** then you shall do to him as he had intended to do to his brother. So you shall purge the evil from your midst. **20** And the rest shall hear and be afraid, and shall never again commit such an evil thing among you. **21** Your eye shall not pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

**LOGOS — Formal Equivalence Analysis, Deuteronomy 19**

**v.1 "cut off the nations" (yakhrit):** The Hiphil of krt, rendered literally. LXX: ἐξολοθρεύσῃ ("utterly destroy"), which is stronger. MT preferred.

**v.3 "prepare the road" (takhin lekha ha-derekh):** Literally "establish/prepare for yourself the road." The instruction to build clear, accessible roads to the refuge cities is rendered straightforwardly. LXX: ἑτοιμάσεις σεαυτῷ ὁδόν, matching MT closely.

**v.4 "from yesterday or the day before" (mittemol shilshom):** This idiomatic Hebrew expression for prior habitual enmity is rendered literally. Standard English idiom would be "previously" or "in the past," but the literal phrasing preserves the flavor of the formula.

**v.5 "the iron head slips from the wood" (nasha'l ha-barzel min ha-ets):** Lit. "the iron slips from the wood." Rendering "iron head" clarifies that the axe-head (not the handle) is what comes loose. LXX: ἐκπεσὸν τὸ σιδήριον ("the iron falling off"), agreeing with MT.

**v.6 "avenger of blood" (go'el ha-dam):** The go'el is the kinsman-redeemer/avenger; "avenger of blood" is the established scholarly rendering and is retained throughout. LXX: ὁ ἀγχιστεύων τὸ αἷμα.

**v.6 "while his heart is hot" (ki yicham levavo):** Retained literally; the idiom conveys passion and urgency in the pursuit. LXX: ὅταν ἐκζητήσῃ ("when he seeks out"), smoothing the idiom.

**v.10 "bloodguilt not be upon you" (dam naki al-qirbeka):** Lit. "innocent blood upon your midst." The phrase "bloodguilt" captures the legal-cultic resonance of dam naki (innocent blood), which defiles the land.

**v.14 "boundary marker" (gevul):** The concrete noun (boundary/border) refers to the stone markers delimiting land parcels. LXX: ὅρια ("boundaries"), similarly concrete. "Move" (tasigg) = to remove, shift; retained literally.

**v.15 "shall be confirmed" (yaqum davar):** Lit. "a matter shall stand/rise." The legal technical force of qum is preserved with "confirmed." LXX: σταθήσεται πᾶν ῥῆμα, matching MT.

**v.16 "malicious witness" (ed khamas):** Ed khamas = "witness of violence/wrongdoing." LXX: μάρτυς ἄδικος ("unjust witness"), choosing a different nuance. MT's khamas implies active violent malice rather than mere injustice; "malicious witness" captures this better.

**v.18 "false witness" (ed shaqer):** Standard term. LXX: μαρτυρεῖ ψευδῆ ("testifies falsely"), verbal rather than nominal.

**v.19 "purge the evil from your midst" (uvi'arta ha-ra' miqirbeka):** The purgation formula recurs throughout Deuteronomy. "Purge" renders the Piel of ba'ar ("burn out/consume"), preserving the sense of actively removing moral corruption from the community.

**v.21 "life for life, eye for eye" (nefesh be-nefesh, ayin be-ayin...):** The lex talionis formula. LOGOS renders each pair literally. This is a legal principle of proportionality, not necessarily literal extraction. LXX follows MT exactly: ψυχὴν ἀντὶ ψυχῆς.

— LOGOS
